title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Language Clinic: Capacity Defense
summary: Optimization starts from intuition, solver and drawing time are combined, huge mazes freeze the event loop, and average latency hides stalls. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capacity defense increment in representative performance and responsiveness report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.
Reduce item churn, batch or diff changes, bound annotations, and retest visual and accessibility correctness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-capacity-defense — Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_performance_profiling_3(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-y355ku
# Competency: maze-capacity-defense.
# Case 4339: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_capacity_defense_y355ku(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_y355ku = "y355ku-uk553y-4339"
    assert evidence_variant_y355ku
    evidence_axes_y355ku = (
        "gridy355ku",
        "topology4339",
        "searchuk553y",
        "eventy355ku4339",
        "access4339y355ku",
        "recoveryuk553y4339",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_y355ku)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Capacity Defense model · Theory Capacity Defense, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Capacity Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Capacity Defense model · Theory Capacity Defense, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3374 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Capacity Defense model · Theory Capacity Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: If a large maze eventually completes it is supported.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-y355ku.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-y355ku(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_capacity_defense_y355ku\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or min\(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?solver_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?render_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
